The main issue discussed is that these sites make people more isolated. Dr. Aric Sigman is mentioned, talking about how increased isolation can lead to health problems like cancer, strokes, heart attacks, and dementia. The article also mentions that face-to-face interactions have decreased since the rise of electronic media, and that online interactions are replacing in-person meetings, affecting both children and adults' social skills. It actually implies that the convenience might be a factor in why people use them, leading to isolation. So B doesn't seem right. C) More isolation is caused by less direct communication.
